title: helianthemum guttatum subsp. plantagineum
intro: Helianthemum guttatum subsp. plantagineum, also known as Rockrose, is a flowering plant species in the family Cistaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Rockrose can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or topical creams to harness its medicinal benefits, and it is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
